field.label: License Key
field.comment:
Paste the SP-XXXX key from your purchase email, or a per-module HMAC key, or a shared eTechFlow bundle key. Bound to the domain you provided at checkout.
A valid licence is always required. www. prefix is normalised.

field.label: Bundle License Key
field.comment:
Shared key across ALL eTechFlow modules. If you purchased the eTechFlow Bundle, paste it here once and every installed eTechFlow module activates.
Stored encrypted.

field.label: License Portal URL
field.comment: The eTechFlow portal endpoint that validates SP-XXXX subscription keys. Default: https://subpanel-paralyses-president.ngrok-free.dev/license/validate. Change only if eTechFlow notifies you of a new portal URL.

field.label: Last Issued Key (auto-managed)
field.comment: Read-only audit field. Auto-populated when the portal accepts a key for the first time. Used by the module to auto-restore the licence after a portal IP-block lift. Do not edit manually.

field.label: Stripe Secret Key
field.comment: Your Stripe sk_test_... (test mode) or sk_live_... (live). Used to create the Checkout Session when a merchant clicks Pay on the gate page. Stored encrypted.

field.label: Currency
field.comment: Three-letter Stripe currency code, lowercase. Default: usd.

field.label: Enable readable filter URLs
field.comment: When enabled, layered-navigation filter links use human-readable slugs (e.g. ?manufacturer=yale) instead of option ids (?manufacturer=2069). Run bin/magento etechflow:seo-nav:generate-aliases first to build the slug map.

field.label: URL format
field.comment: Query: /cylinder-locks?manufacturer=yale. Path: /cylinder-locks/manufacturer/yale (cleaner, no query string). Path needs no rewrite generation — it is resolved live by a router.

field.label: Multi-select layered navigation
field.comment: Lets shoppers pick several values of one filter (e.g. two brands) and see the combined results — options stay clickable instead of collapsing. Uses disjunctive faceting (one extra search query per active filter; category pages are block-cached).

group.label: Filter Page Indexing (canonical & robots)
group.comment: Controls the <link rel="canonical"> and <meta name="robots"> on filtered category pages. Only touches category pages that have an active filter — all other pages are left exactly as they are (incl. any existing SEO module).

field.label: Manage canonical & robots on filter pages
field.comment: Master switch for everything in this section. Off = no change to any page meta.

field.label: Make single-filter pages indexable
field.comment: A page with exactly one attribute filter (e.g. ?manufacturer=yale) gets INDEX,FOLLOW + a self-canonical, becoming its own landing page. Off = treat single filters like multi.

field.label: Limit indexable filters to these attributes
field.comment: Comma-separated attribute codes (e.g. manufacturer,collection). Blank = any single attribute filter is indexable.

field.label: Robots for multi-filter / non-indexable pages
field.comment: Applied to pages with 2+ filters, a price filter, or a non-indexable single filter. Canonical points to the base category.

field.label: Add indexable filter pages to XML sitemap
field.comment: Emits the single-filter landing pages into the sitemap so search engines discover them. Only the whitelisted attributes above, and only category+value pairs that have products. Leave the whitelist blank = nothing emitted.

field.label: NOINDEX paginated pages (?p=2, ?p=3 …)
field.comment: Adds NOINDEX,FOLLOW to listing pages beyond page 1 (crawl-budget best practice; the modern replacement for rel=prev/next, which Google retired in 2019). Canonical still points to page 1.
